description: Adopt cc-safety-net@1.0.6 as the cross-provider destructive-command guard (claude/codex/pi), keep secret-exposure hooks, retire pi's dead RISKY_BASH branch, and keep harness automation working via a file-invoked git-maintenance script. Authoritative references: .oh/tasks/cc-safety-net/prd.md (v2.1) and install-decision.md.

userStories.title: Non-interactive install spike
userStories.description: As the harness maintainer, I need a proven non-interactive install mechanism per provider before wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: Decision table with install mechanism, files touched, offline safety, and env modes per provider exists at .oh/tasks/cc-safety-net/install-decision.md
userStories.notes: Completed pre-build (empirical spike, 2026-07-19). install-decision.md is authoritative for all later stories.

userStories.title: Binary + env plumbing (prerequisite wave)
userStories.description: As the harness maintainer, I want the pinned binary baked into the image and mode env wired so later stories can exercise the real hook.
acceptanceCriteria: NEW standalone unconditional 'RUN npm install -g cc-safety-net@1.0.6' instruction in .devcontainer/Dockerfile AFTER the existing agent-CLI install block (~line 130), following the separate pi-coding-agent RUN-block pattern — NOT threaded into the AGENTS/PKG loop; no existing lines removed (protected path, additive-only override declared in prd.md)
CC_SAFETY_NET_STRICT=1 and CC_SAFETY_NET_WORKTREE=1 set in .devcontainer/docker-compose.yml / entrypoint env; no PARANOID vars, no DEBUG, CC_SAFETY_NET_OFF left unset
docker-compose.yml mounts ~/.cc-safety-net as a named volume alongside the existing per-tool volumes

.oh/scripts/link-providers.sh gains a NEW binary check-kind (command -v cc-safety-net + version match against 1.0.6) alongside required_execs, failing via the existing fail path when missing/mismatched UNLESS CC_SAFETY_NET_OFF=1 (then warn and continue); entrypoint.sh and link-providers.sh changes are additive-only (protected paths)
Boot performs zero npm-registry access for this feature (local presence checks only)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: Claude wiring
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want cc-safety-net enforcing on claude despite bypassPermissions.
acceptanceCriteria: .claude/settings.json PreToolUse matcher "Bash" hooks[] gains exactly: {"type":"command","command":"sh -c '[ \"$CC_SAFETY_NET_OFF\" = \"1\" ] || exec cc-safety-net hook --claude-code'"}
All existing hook entries byte-for-byte unchanged (only this file touched)

Headless verification: piping {"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git reset --hard HEAD"}} through the configured command yields permissionDecision "deny"; a benign command passes; with CC_SAFETY_NET_OFF=1 the wrapper exits 0 with no output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: Codex wiring
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want cc-safety-net enforcing on codex (approval_policy=never).
acceptanceCriteria: .codex/hooks.json gains the same guard-wrapped command as a second PreToolUse/Bash entry; existing deny-env-dump.sh entry unchanged; config.toml untouched (hooks=true already set); only hooks.json touched
Same headless block/pass/kill-switch verification as US-003
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: Pi wiring + path-guard refactor
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want pi guarded deterministically by the cc-safety-net extension, retiring the dead RISKY_BASH branch and keeping SENSITIVE_PATHS.
acceptanceCriteria: "npm:cc-safety-net@1.0.6" added to packages in .pi/settings.json and as a pinned dependency in .pi/npm/package.json (hand-edit; pi install rejected per install-decision.md)
RISKY_BASH array + bash branch removed from .pi/extensions/path-guard.ts; SENSITIVE_PATHS branch and /guard command retained; /guard listing text updated to name cc-safety-net as the bash guard
path-guard.test.ts updated: risky-bash describe block removed, headless + sensitive-path cases retained; path-guard.property.test.ts untouched (it has no RISKY_BASH cases)
Both pi extension test suites pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Flagged 2 iterations in prd.md: iteration 1 = wire, iteration 2 = refactor + tests. Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: Harness destructive-git compatibility (git-maintenance.sh)
userStories.description: As the harness's own automation, I need my legitimate destructive git operations to keep working under the guard via file-invoked scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: New .oh/scripts/git-maintenance.sh with subcommands reset-hard <ref>, clean, branch-delete <branch>, worktree-remove <path>, push-force <remote> <branch>; each refuses to run outside the repo and logs what it did
With the hook active, 'bash .oh/scripts/git-maintenance.sh reset-hard HEAD' succeeds while inline 'git reset --hard HEAD' is denied (verified headless via the US-003 hook command)

.oh/crons/cleanup-tasks.md inline 'git worktree remove --force' / 'git branch -D' calls (lines ~54-55, ~66, plus prose references) rewritten to git-maintenance.sh invocations; behavior preserved, no cleanup step removed (protected path, override declared in prd.md); grep across all .oh/crons/*.md confirms no other inline destructive-git remains

.oh/skills/git/SKILL.md, .oh/skills/watchdog/SKILL.md, .oh/skills/worktrees/SKILL.md reference the script for destructive git in agent contexts and state the corrected scope rule: only non-agent-mediated invocations (raw scheduler/tmux shell scripts) bypass PreToolUse hooks; agent-driven crons do NOT
Remaining blocked classes (rm -rf escalations, dd/mkfs/shred, find -delete, interpreter one-liners, git stash clear) documented as having NO script escape hatch (kill-switch only)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: Eval probe cc-safety-net-wiring.sh
userStories.description: As the harness maintainer, I want a deterministic probe so a wiring regression turns the eval suite red.
acceptanceCriteria: New .oh/evals/probes/cc-safety-net-wiring.sh modeled on devtcp-hook.sh: 3-state (exit 0 PASS / 1 REGRESSION / 2 SKIPPED), '# tier:' header
Config-file assertions (claude + codex hook entries with guard wrapper, pi pin in both files, Dockerfile pin line, compose env vars) are repo-static and NEVER skip — absence is REGRESSION
Only the live-binary block test (cc-safety-net on PATH answering deny for 'git reset --hard HEAD') may SKIP, and only when the binary is absent outside the built sandbox image
/eval suite green with the probe
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: Documentation + operator runbook
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the layered guard model, override paths, and rollout steps documented.
acceptanceCriteria: .oh/docs/security-considerations.md: cc-safety-net as destructive-command layer; secret guards as complementary layer; per-provider coverage matrix incl. codex single-hook state and pi headless SENSITIVE_PATHS gap; 'a prompt asks; a hook enforces' doctrine

Operator runbook: CC_SAFETY_NET_OFF=1 override (new processes only) and git-maintenance.sh routing; the PI EXCEPTION stated in the runbook itself (env var does not affect pi; disable = remove package entry from .pi/settings.json + restart session)

Rollout/restart step: exact commands to restart long-lived sessions post-merge (cron-system, autopilot-*, client-slack-pi tmux sessions); audit-trail location ~/.cc-safety-net/logs/<session_id>.jsonl noted (persisted via US-002 volume, secrets redacted)
.oh/docs/oh-directory-layout.md and .oh/docs/harness-manifest.md list the new files; hermes gap documented
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.

userStories.title: CHANGELOG + decision record
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the change logged and the fate-of-hooks decision captured.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG entry per /git conventions

.oh/tasks/cc-safety-net/decision.md records: ADOPT @1.0.6 (pin + sha512 integrity documented for manual re-pin audits) / KEEP secret guards / KEEP SENSITIVE_PATHS with pre-existing headless gap named as future work / RETIRE RISKY_BASH ('dead code in headless and TUI modes today') / DOCUMENT hermes gap / manual re-pin policy with re-validation steps / script-file evasion route accepted (Docker is the boundary)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified by per-unit audit 2026-07-19 (AUDIT-PASS); CI green on PR #655.
